bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Position: Defender 
  • Daughter of  Landon and Sarah Guilday
  • In the seventh grade, she developed a brain tumor, resulting in her losing sight in her right eye.
  • Hobbies include golf, camping and fishing
Biographical Information

Rory Guilday grew up in a suburb of downtown Minneapolis and started playing hockey at age 5. In seventh grade, Guilday developed a brain tumor, resulting in her losing sight in her right eye. Guilday recalls: “Very last day of seventh grade they’re like, you need to start chemotherapy because we don’t know want it to affect your other eye.”  She was sidelined from any contact sports for nearly a year and endured intensive chemotherapy.

Guilday regained lost muscle and balance and returned to skating, finishing off her high school career by winning the Herb Brooks Award in 2021, given annually to a Minnesota State tournament participant who strongly represents the values, characteristics, and traits that defined legendary hockey coach Herb Brooks. 

Guilday attended Cornell University, where she finished her career as a two-time First Team All-Ivy League player. She was selected in the first round (fifth overall) in the 2025 PWHL Draft by the Ottawa Charge.

Guilday made her debut for the U.S. women’s hockey team at the 2022 IIHF World Championships.

World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2024 – silver (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2022, 2023, 2024
  • Medals: 3 (1 gold, 2 silver)
    • Gold – 2023 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2024 (Team - Women); 2022 (Team - Women)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Women)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
    • Born with cerebral palsy, which affects her motor skills
    • Despite this, she displayed an early interest in sports and participated in various athletic activities during her childhood
    • Starting with Tae Kwon Do, Guimaraes is a 2nd Degree Black Belt
    • Guimaraes is also a Para Indoor Rock Climbing Medalist, CP Soccer MVP, and World Cup Winner
    • Attended Cranford High School in New Jersey, where she made both soccer and Varsity track teams
      • Was also a CP Soccer Women's World Cup Winner and MVP for the 2022 CP Soccer World Cup held in Salou, Spain, as part of the first-ever team fielded by US SoccerHas been an advocate for disability sports and often shares her journey to inspire othersEnjoys spending time with her family and friends, and she is passionate about promoting inclusivity in sportsIs the proud owner of a tripod rescue that serves as a service animal to support her in everyday activitiesOutside of athletics, she is pursuing a degree in exercise physiologyA two-time published author, she is also working on her third book which is still in development

Adnan Gutic

Quick Facts

Adnan was born in Bosnia and escaped the war with his parents when he was a child. Growing up in St. Louis, MO, Adnan attended the Missouri School for the Blind where he excelled in wrestling. He also wrestled for Linwood University. While in college, he picked up judo to augment his wrestling. In 2009, USA Judo held visually impaired divisions for the first time and Adnan won the 81kg division. Adnan made his first IBSA World Championship Team in 2010 and was also a member of the 2011 IBSA World Games Team. He is currently ranked #1 in the United States at 81kg and will be representing the US at the 2015 Para Pan American Games in Toronto, Canada. Adnan currently lives in St. Louis, MO and trains at the White Dragon Judo Club.

Major Judo Awards:

  • 2015 IBSA World Games Team Member, 81 Kg
  • 2014 IBSA World Team Member, 81Kg
  • 2014 US National Championships – Gold, Visually Impaired Men, 81Kg
  • 2013 IBSA Pan American Championships – Gold, Visually Impaired Men, 81Kg
  • 2011 US Open – Gold, Visually Impaired Men 73Kg
  • 2011 President’s Cup – Gold, Visually Impaired Men 73Kg
  • 2011 IBSA World Team Member, 73Kg
  • 2010 US National Championships – Gold, Visually Impaired Men, 73Kg
  • 2010 IBSA World Team Member, 73Kg
  • 2009 US National Championships – Gold, Visually Impaired Men, 81Kg
